name: getFlagShowExoplanets
description: Returns whether exoplanetary systems are displayed on the sky as markers.
returnDescription: true if exoplanet systems are visible, false if hidden

name: setFlagShowExoplanets
description: Shows or hides exoplanetary systems on the sky. Systems are displayed as markers around host stars.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: true to show exoplanet systems, false to hide

name: showExoplanets
description: Property that controls whether exoplanet systems are displayed. This is the recommended way to read/write this setting.
returnDescription: true if exoplanet systems are visible, false if hidden

name: getFlagShowExoplanetsDesignations
description: Returns whether designations of exoplanetary systems are displayed as labels.
returnDescription: true if designations are visible, false if hidden

name: setFlagShowExoplanetsDesignations
description: Shows or hides designations of exoplanetary systems (e.g., 'HD 209458', 'Kepler-10').

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: true to show designations, false to hide

name: flagShowExoplanetsDesignations
description: Property that controls whether exoplanet system designations are displayed.
example: core.setStelProperty('Exoplanets.flagShowExoplanetsDesignations', true);

name: getFlagShowExoplanetsNumbers
description: Returns whether the number of exoplanets in each system is displayed next to the marker.
returnDescription: true if planet numbers are visible, false if hidden

name: setFlagShowExoplanetsNumbers
description: Shows or hides the number of exoplanets in each system next to the marker.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: true to show planet numbers, false to hide

name: flagShowExoplanetsNumbers
description: Property that controls whether exoplanet counts are displayed.
returnDescription: true if planet numbers are visible
example: core.setStelProperty('Exoplanets.flagShowExoplanetsNumbers', true);

name: getDisplayMode
description: Returns whether distribution mode is enabled for exoplanets (showing density distribution rather than individual markers).
returnDescription: true if distribution mode is enabled, false if not

name: setDisplayMode
description: Enables or disables distribution mode for exoplanets (showing density distribution rather than individual markers).

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to enable distribution mode, false to disable

name: flagDisplayMode
description: Property that controls whether exoplanet distribution mode is enabled.
returnDescription: true if distribution mode is enabled
example: core.setStelProperty('Exoplanets.flagDisplayMode', true);

name: getTimelineMode
description: Returns whether timeline mode is enabled (showing only exoplanets discovered by the current simulation date).
returnDescription: true if timeline mode is enabled, false if all systems shown

compatibility.notes: Shows exoplanets as they were discovered historically

name: setTimelineMode
description: Enables or disables timeline mode, showing only exoplanets that were discovered by the current simulation date.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to enable timeline mode, false to disable

name: flagTimelineMode
description: Property that controls whether timeline mode is enabled.
returnDescription: true if timeline mode is enabled
example: core.setStelProperty('Exoplanets.flagTimelineMode', true);

name: getHabitableMode
description: Returns whether habitable mode is enabled (showing only exoplanetary systems with potentially habitable exoplanets).
returnDescription: true if only habitable systems are shown, false if all systems shown

compatibility.notes: Based on data from the Habitable Exoplanets Catalog (PHL)

name: setHabitableMode
description: Filters exoplanet systems to show only those with potentially habitable exoplanets.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to show only habitable systems, false to show all

name: flagHabitableMode
description: Property that controls whether only habitable exoplanet systems are displayed.
returnDescription: true if only habitable systems are shown
example: core.setStelProperty('Exoplanets.flagHabitableMode', true);

name: getMarkerColor
description: Returns the color used for exoplanetary system markers. Default is light green (0.4, 0.9, 0.5).
returnDescription: RGB color vector with values from 0.0 to 1.0
example:
var color = Exoplanets.getMarkerColor();
core.debug("Exoplanet marker color: RGB(" + color[0] + ", " + color[1] + ", " + color[2] + ")");

name: setMarkerColor
description: Sets the color for exoplanetary system markers.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: RGB color vector with values between 0.0 and 1.0

name: markerColor
description: ⚠️ Property that returns the exoplanet marker color. For reading, use core.getStelProperty(). For writing, use Exoplanets.setMarkerColor().

compatibility.notes: Q_PROPERTY exposed to scripting (read via core.getStelProperty, write via method)

name: getHabitableColor
description: Returns the color used for potentially habitable exoplanet systems (when habitable mode is enabled). Default is orange (1.0, 0.5, 0.0).
returnDescription: RGB color vector with values from 0.0 to 1.0
example:
var color = Exoplanets.getHabitableColor();
core.debug("Habitable exoplanet color: RGB(" + color[0] + ", " + color[1] + ", " + color[2] + ")");

name: setHabitableColor
description: Sets the color for potentially habitable exoplanet systems.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: RGB color vector with values between 0.0 and 1.0

name: habitableColor
description: ⚠️ Property that returns the habitable exoplanet marker color. For reading, use core.getStelProperty(). For writing, use Exoplanets.setHabitableColor().

compatibility.notes: Q_PROPERTY exposed to scripting (read via core.getStelProperty, write via method)

example: var colorStr = core.getStelProperty('Exoplanets.habitableColor');

name: getCountPlanetarySystems
description: Returns the number of planetary systems (stars with exoplanets) currently loaded in the catalog.
example:
var systems = Exoplanets.getCountPlanetarySystems();
core.debug("Planetary systems: " + systems);

name: getCountAllExoplanets
description: Returns the total number of confirmed exoplanets currently loaded in the catalog.
example:
var count = Exoplanets.getCountAllExoplanets();
core.debug("Total confirmed exoplanets: " + count);

name: getCountHabitableExoplanets
description: Returns the number of potentially habitable exoplanets currently loaded in the catalog.

compatibility.notes: Based on PHL's Habitable Exoplanets Catalog

example:
var habitable = Exoplanets.getCountHabitableExoplanets();
core.debug("Potentially habitable exoplanets: " + habitable);

name: getUpdatesEnabled
description: Returns whether automatic updates of the exoplanet catalog from the internet are enabled.
returnDescription: true if automatic updates are enabled, false if disabled

name: setUpdatesEnabled
description: Enables or disables automatic updates of the exoplanet catalog from the internet.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to enable automatic updates, false to disable

name: updateJSON
description: Manually triggers an immediate update of the exoplanet catalog JSON file from the configured online source (default: https://www.stellarium.org/json/exoplanets.json).

name: getLastUpdate
description: Returns the date and time of the last successful exoplanet catalog update.
returnDescription: Date and time of last catalog update
example:
var lastUpdate = Exoplanets.getLastUpdate();
core.debug("Last exoplanet catalog update: " + lastUpdate.toString());

name: getUpdateFrequencyHours
description: Returns the frequency of automatic catalog updates in hours. Default is 72 hours (3 days).
example:
var freq = Exoplanets.getUpdateFrequencyHours();
core.debug("Update frequency: " + freq + " hours");

name: setUpdateFrequencyHours
description: Sets the frequency of automatic catalog updates in hours.

name: getSecondsToUpdate
description: Returns the number of seconds until the next scheduled automatic catalog update.
example:
var secs = Exoplanets.getSecondsToUpdate();
core.debug("Next update in: " + secs + " seconds");

name: getUpdateState
description: Returns the current update state. Values: 0=Updating, 1=CompleteNoUpdates, 2=CompleteUpdates, 3=DownloadError, 4=OtherError.

name: getEnableAtStartup
description: Returns whether the Exoplanets plugin should be enabled automatically when Stellarium starts.
returnDescription: true if enabled at startup, false otherwise

name: setEnableAtStartup
description: Sets whether the Exoplanets plugin should be enabled automatically when Stellarium starts.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to enable at startup, false to disable

name: getFlagShowExoplanetsButton
description: Returns whether the Exoplanets plugin toolbar button is visible in the Stellarium GUI.
returnDescription: true if button is visible, false if hidden

name: setFlagShowExoplanetsButton
description: Shows or hides the Exoplanets plugin toolbar button in the Stellarium GUI.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: true to show button, false to hide

name: restoreDefaults
description: Restores all Exoplanets plugin settings and catalog to their default values. This will reset visibility, colors, filter settings, and replace the catalog file.

name: getByID
description: Returns an exoplanetary system object by its designation (e.g., 'Kepler-10', 'HD 209458', 'TRAPPIST-1').
returnDescription: Exoplanet system object, or null if not found

compatibility.notes: Returns an ExoplanetP object with methods like getEnglishName(), getExoplanetsCount(), getHabitableCount(), etc.

name: getAllExoplanetarySystems
description: Returns the list of all exoplanetary system objects currently loaded in the catalog.

name: searchByID
description: Searches for an exoplanetary system by its designation. Returns a StelObjectP that can be used with core.moveToObject() and other object functions.
returnDescription: Exoplanet system as StelObject, or null if not found

compatibility.notes: Alternative to getByID() that returns StelObjectP

name: searchByName
description: Searches for an exoplanetary system by its English name or designation.
returnDescription: Exoplanet system object, or null if not found

name: listAllObjects
description: Returns a list of all exoplanet system objects with their names. This can be used for auto-completion and search.

parameters.name: inEnglish
parameters.description: true to list English names, false for translated names

example:
var objects = Exoplanets.listAllObjects(false);
core.debug("Total exoplanet systems in catalog: " + objects.length);

name: getName
description: Returns the display name of the Exoplanets plugin module.
example:
var name = Exoplanets.getName();
core.debug("Module: " + name);

name: getStelObjectType
description: Returns the object type identifier for exoplanet systems. Used by StelObjectMgr to identify objects from this module.
example:
var objType = Exoplanets.getStelObjectType();
core.debug("Object type: " + objType); // Returns 'Exoplanet'

name: setCurrentTemperatureScale
description: Sets the temperature scale for displaying exoplanet temperatures. Values: 0=Kelvin, 1=Celsius, 2=Fahrenheit.

name: getCurrentTemperatureScale
description: Returns the current temperature scale for exoplanet display. 0=Kelvin, 1=Celsius, 2=Fahrenheit.

name: getCurrentTemperatureScaleKey
description: Returns the key name of the current temperature scale ('Kelvin', 'Celsius', or 'Fahrenheit').
example:
var scaleKey = Exoplanets.getCurrentTemperatureScaleKey();
core.debug("Temperature scale: " + scaleKey);

name: setCurrentTemperatureScaleKey
description: Sets the temperature scale by its key name ('Kelvin', 'Celsius', or 'Fahrenheit').
example: Exoplanets.setCurrentTemperatureScaleKey("Celsius");
